---
title: "Quarto 7 — Projects, Themes, Publishing"
subtitle: "From single files to deployed sites"
author: "R. Burke Squires"
institute: "NIEHS"
date: today
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, niehs-theme.scss]
    logo: ../../images/niehs-logo.png
    footer: "R Markdown + Quarto · NIEHS · 2026"
    slide-number: true
    chalkboard: true
    code-overflow: wrap
---

## Goals — 20 minutes

Three short topics:

::: {.incremental}
- **Projects** (~7 min) — what they are, four types
- **Themes and CSS** (~7 min) — make it look like a NIEHS report
- **Publishing** (~6 min) — get it to a URL
:::

# Projects {.section-divider}

## A project is a folder + `_quarto.yml`

```
my-project/
├── _quarto.yml          ← Required
├── index.qmd
├── chapters/
│   ├── ch01.qmd
│   └── ch02.qmd
├── references.bib
├── styles.css
└── _site/               ← Rendered output
```

::: {.fragment}
This workshop is a Quarto **website** project. Look at the root `_quarto.yml`.
:::

## Four project types

| Type | Use when |
|------|----------|
| `default` | Shared resources, independent renders |
| `website` | Multi-page site (this workshop) |
| `book` | Sequential pagination + bibliography |
| `manuscript` | Article + supplementary notebooks |

::: {.fragment}
**Quarto Manuscripts (1.4+)** — built for the scientific article use case. Worth investigating for NIEHS publishing workflows.
:::

# Themes & CSS {.section-divider}

## 25 Bootswatch themes — pick one

```yaml
format:
  html:
    theme: cosmo
    # or: flatly, journal, lumen, paper,
    # simplex, sandstone, spacelab, united, yeti,
    # cerulean, cyborg, darkly, ...
```

::: {.fragment}
**R Markdown parity:** the same theme names work in both.
:::

## Dark mode toggle

```yaml
format:
  html:
    theme:
      light: cosmo
      dark: darkly
```

::: {.fragment}
Quarto adds a sun/moon button to the navbar.
:::

## Custom CSS

```yaml
format:
  html:
    theme: [cosmo, styles.css]
```

```{.css}
:root {
  --niehs-green: #205C40;
}

h1, h2, h3 {
  color: var(--niehs-green);
}

blockquote {
  border-left: 4px solid var(--niehs-green);
  padding-left: 1em;
}
```

## SCSS for deep customization

```{.scss}
/*-- scss:defaults --*/
$primary: #205C40;
$body-color: #2B2B2B;

/*-- scss:rules --*/
.callout-note .callout-title {
  background-color: rgba(32, 92, 64, 0.1);
}
```

::: {.fragment}
The slide decks use exactly this pattern — see `niehs-theme.scss`.
:::

## Conditional output

```{.r}
if (knitr::is_html_output()) {
  DT::datatable(my_data)   # interactive
} else {
  kable(my_data)            # static
}
```

::: {.fragment}
Same `knitr::is_*_output()` functions you knew from R Markdown.
:::

# Publishing {.section-divider}

## Five reasonable targets

::: {.incremental}
- **Target 0** — render + share HTML (no account needed)
- **Target 1** — Quarto Pub (free, public)
- **Target 2** — GitHub Pages
- **Target 3** — Posit Connect Cloud
- **Target 4** — any static host (Netlify, S3, internal NIEHS server)
:::

## Target 0 — local + share

```bash
quarto render
```

::: {.incremental}
- HTMLs are self-contained
- Email them, or drop on a shared drive
- Upload to an internal NIEHS server
- **Often the right answer for internal-only work**
:::

## Target 1 — Quarto Pub

```bash
quarto publish quarto-pub
```

::: {.fragment}
Free, public at `username.quarto.pub`. First time browser auth, then one command forever.
:::

## Target 2 — GitHub Pages

```bash
quarto publish gh-pages
```

::: {.fragment}
Site at `username.github.io/repo-name`. Requires a Git repo with GitHub remote.
:::

## Don't confuse names

::: {.callout-warning}
## posit.cloud ≠ Posit Connect Cloud

**posit.cloud** — where you *develop* (this workshop).

**Posit Connect Cloud** — where you *publish*.

Publishing from posit.cloud was **deprecated in Quarto 1.8**. Replacement is Posit Connect Cloud (separate product, similar name).
:::

# Beyond today {.section-divider}

## Topics worth exploring later

::: {.incremental}
- **Quarto Dashboards** — fluid-layout interactive dashboards (1.4+)
- **Quarto Extensions** — community shortcodes, filters, custom formats
- **Shiny + Quarto** — interactive documents backed by Shiny
- **Custom Pandoc filters** — when you need to bend the rendering pipeline
- **Quarto Manuscripts** — submitting to scientific journals
:::

::: {.fragment}
All linked from <https://quarto.org/docs>.
:::

# Hands-on {.section-divider}

## Notebook Quarto 7

::: {.callout-tip}
Open `afternoon_quarto/07_projects_themes_publishing.qmd`. Exercises 7.1 and 7.2.
:::

20 minutes. Then capstone.
